title: Repr
description: Representation descriptor (the paradigm-in-the-type). Faithful JSON projection of RFC-0001 §4.1. The four paradigm KINDS are closed in the kernel (a fifth requires an RFC+ADR); the parameter registries (dtype, model, pack scheme) are open. Two values share a type only if kind and semantic parameters match; Binary{8} and Ternary{6} are unrelated types.

Nat.description: A positive width/dimension. Zero-width reprs are not well-formed.

ScalarKind.description: RFC-0001 §4.1: an EXTENSIBLE registry. Enumerated here as the initial set; adding a member is a registry change, not an RFC. dtype stays in the Dense type because precision is semantically significant (it bounds embedding error).

SparsityClass.description: RFC-0001 §4.1 / RFC-0003 §5. The DECLARED sparsity class is a static refinement {v | activeCount v <= max_active}. Observed sparsity is runtime Meta.sparsity, not here.

model.description: ModelId resolved against the VSA model registry, populated by the VSA submodule (ADR-008), not the kernel. E.g. MAP-I, MAP-B, BSC, HRR, FHRR, Sparse.
